pr_summary: This PR adds new functionality to the codebase.
verdict_reason: The review found critical-severity issues requiring attention.

findings.title: SQL Injection via f-string interpolation
findings.description: This line is vulnerable to SQL injection because it directly interpolates user-supplied values into a SQL query string. An attacker could manipulate the transaction_id to inject malicious SQL code, potentially leading to data breaches or system compromise.
findings.suggestion: Use parameterized queries instead, e.g., query = "SELECT * FROM transactions WHERE user_id = %s AND transaction_id = %s"; db.execute(query, (user_id, transaction_id))

findings.line_content: db.execute("UPDATE transactions SET status='refunded' WHERE id=" + str(transaction['id']))
findings.title: SQL Injection via string concatenation
findings.description: This line is vulnerable to SQL injection because it directly concatenates user-supplied values into a SQL query string. An attacker could manipulate the transaction['id'] to inject malicious SQL code, potentially leading to data breaches or system compromise.
findings.suggestion: Use parameterized queries instead, e.g., query = "UPDATE transactions SET status='refunded' WHERE id = %s"; db.execute(query, (transaction['id'],))

findings.title: Hardcoded secret key
findings.description: This line exposes a live Stripe secret key, which is a critical security risk. An attacker could use this key to access sensitive payment information or perform unauthorized transactions.
findings.suggestion: Store the secret key securely using environment variables or a secrets management system, e.g., os.environ['STRIPE_SECRET_KEY']

findings.title: NULL DEREFERENCE
findings.description: The process_refund function does not check if the request['email'] is None before trying to use it. If the request['email'] is None, this will cause a NULL DEREFERENCE error.
findings.suggestion: Add a check for None: send_notification(request.get('email', ''), f"Refund of {refund_amount} processed")

findings.title: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) via unescaped user-controlled value
findings.description: This line is vulnerable to XSS because it directly inserts a user-controlled value into an email template without proper escaping. An attacker could manipulate the message to inject malicious HTML code, potentially leading to phishing attacks or session hijacking.
findings.suggestion: Use HTML-escaping to sanitize the message, e.g., body = template.replace('{{message}}', html.escape(message))

positive_observations: The code follows a consistent structure.
Function naming is clear and descriptive.
